coredata.dc:title: A novel multifunctional Tb-MOF fluorescent probe displaying excellent abilities for highly selective detection of Fe3+, Cr2O7 2− and acetylacetone
coredata.dc:creator: Li, Jiaxin
Yu, Baoqiu
Fan, Linhan
Wang, Lin
Zhao, Yichen
Sun, Changyan
Li, Wenjun
Chang, Zhidong
coredata.dc:description: Metal-organic frameworks (MOFs) show the pre-eminence over other traditional fluorescent probes in the detection of water contaminants. Here, a novel lanthanide metal-organic framework Tb-MOF was successfully constructed by anchoring Tb3+ and 4,4'-(4-amino-1,2,4-triazol-3,5-diyl)dibenzoic acid (H2atdbc) which exhibited high chemical stability and water stability. Furthermore, sensing experiments showed that Tb-MOF could be used as a multifunctional probe to detect Fe3+ (LOD ​= ​5.01 ​× ​10-7 ​mol ​L-1, Ksv ​= ​1.28 ​× ​104 ​L ​mol-1), Cr2O7 2− (LOD ​= ​2.92 ​× ​10-6 ​mol ​L-1, Ksv ​= ​8454.37 ​L ​mol-1) and acetylacetone (LOD ​= ​5.59 ​× ​10-4 ​mol ​L-1, Ksv ​= ​12.32 ​L ​mol-1) separately. PXRD, ICP-MS, IR and UV–vis spectra were conducted to explore the detection mechanisms. The experiments showed that the detection mechanism for Fe3+ was energy transfer and ion exchange, and the detection mechanisms for Cr2O7 2− and acetylacetone were energy transfer.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Metal-organic frameworks
4,4'-(4-amino-1,2,4-triazol-3,5-diyl)dibenzoic acid
Multifunctional probes
Fe3+
Cr2O7 2-

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: The solid-state fluorescent emission spectrum of Tb-MOF (Ex ​= ​325 ​nm). Inset: Tb-MOF luminescence under ultraviolet light.

(a) Fluorescence intensity of Tb-MOF in different aqueous solutions of pH ​= ​1–11 and its luminescence under ultraviolet light (b) Fluorescence emission spectra of Tb-MOF in different aqueous solutions of pH ​= ​1–11.

(a) The fluorescence intensity of Tb-MOF dispersed in different metal ions. (b) The fluorescence intensity of Tb-MOF (5D4-7F5) dispersed in different corresponding aqueous solutions of metal ions (blue) and subsequent addition of Fe3+ (red). (c) The fluorescence intensity of Tb-MOF dispersed in Fe3+ aqueous solutions of different concentrations. (d) The correlation between the Fluorescence intensity of Tb-MOF (5D4-7F5) and the concentration of Fe3+ (Inset: The linear correlation between the Fluorescence intensity of Tb-MOF (5D4-7F5) and the concentration of Fe3+. (For interpretation of the references to color in this figure legend, the reader is referred to the Web version of this article.)

(a) The fluorescence intensity of Tb-MOF dispersed in different anions; (b) The fluorescence intensity of Tb-MOF (5D4-7F5) dispersed in different corresponding aqueous solutions of anions (blue) and subsequent addition of Cr2O7 2− (red). (c) The fluorescence intensity of Tb-MOF dispersed in different concentration of Cr2O7 2− aqueous solutions s. (d) The linear correlation between the fluorescence intensity of Tb-MOF (5D4-7F5) and the concentration of Cr2O7 2−. (For interpretation of the references to color in this figure legend, the reader is referred to the Web version of this article.)

(a) The fluorescence intensity of Tb-MOF dispersed in different organic solvents; (b) The fluorescence intensity of Tb-MOF (5D4-7F5) dispersed in different corresponding organic solvents (blue) and subsequent addition of acetylacetone (red). (c) The fluorescence intensity of adding different volumes of acetylacetone to the CH3OH:H2O ​= ​1:1 solution (d) The linear correlation between the fluorescence intensity of Tb-MOF (5D4-7F5) and the concentration of acetylacetone. (For interpretation of the references to color in this figure legend, the reader is referred to the Web version of this article.)
The UV–Vis adsorption spectra of Fe3+, Cr2O7 2−, acetylacetone and the excitation spectrum of Tb-MOF.

head.ce:title: A novel multifunctional Tb-MOF fluorescent probe displaying excellent abilities for highly selective detection of Fe3+, Cr2O7 2− and acetylacetone

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: School of Chemistry and Biological Engineering, University of Science and Technology Beijing, Beijing, 100083, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: School of Chemistry and Biological Engineering
University of Science and Technology Beijing

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: School of Chemistry and Biological Engineering, University of Science and Technology Beijing, Beijing, 100083, China

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Metal-organic frameworks (MOFs) show the pre-eminence over other traditional fluorescent probes in the detection of water contaminants. Here, a novel lanthanide metal-organic framework Tb-MOF was successfully constructed by anchoring Tb3+ and 4,4'-(4-amino-1,2,4-triazol-3,5-diyl)dibenzoic acid (H2atdbc) which exhibited high chemical stability and water stability. Furthermore, sensing experiments showed that Tb-MOF could be used as a multifunctional probe to detect Fe3+ (LOD ​= ​5.01 ​× ​10-7 ​mol ​L-1, Ksv ​= ​1.28 ​× ​104 ​L ​mol-1), Cr2O7 2− (LOD ​= ​2.92 ​× ​10-6 ​mol ​L-1, Ksv ​= ​8454.37 ​L ​mol-1) and acetylacetone (LOD ​= ​5.59 ​× ​10-4 ​mol ​L-1, Ksv ​= ​12.32 ​L ​mol-1) separately. PXRD, ICP-MS, IR and UV–vis spectra were conducted to explore the detection mechanisms. The experiments showed that the detection mechanism for Fe3+ was energy transfer and ion exchange, and the detection mechanisms for Cr2O7 2− and acetylacetone were energy transfer.

This manuscript reports the synthesis of a novel Tb-MOF and its application as a multifunctional fluorescent probe to detect Fe3+, Cr2O7 2− and acetylacetone.
Image 1

ce:section.ce:para: In recent years, metal-organic frameworks (MOFs) have attracted much attention in chemistry and materials because of their highly adjustable structure and size [1]. Their special structures make them widely used in the field of gas separation, catalysis, magnetic materials, supercapacitors, sensors and so on [2–10]. Particularly, fluorescent metal-organic frameworks (FMOFs) have gradually developed into promising functional materials and have been widely used as fluorescent probes, which are formed by metal ions or ion clusters and organic ligands with aromatic rings and large π conjugated structures [11,12]. Lanthanide metal ions, especially Tb3+ and Eu3+ ions, which have large stoke shifts, high color purity and long fluorescence lifetime, are often used to construct fluorescent probes [13]. For example, Yu et al. synthesized two three-dimensional porous structures Eu-MOF and Tb-MOF which can rapidly and simultaneously detect Fe3+ and Cr2O7 2−/CrO4 2− ions in water [14]. Yang et al. prepared Eu3+@Ni-MOF which could act as a fluorescent probe for aspartic acid [15]. Zhang et al. encapsulated formamide-derived N-doped carbon dots (FA-CDs) in the cavities of Eu(III)-based MOFs and designed a novel ratiometric fluorescent sensor for ultrasensitive and visual quantitative detection of Ag+ with naked eyes [16].

With the development of science and industry, emissions of pollutants are also increasing, which would harm environment and organisms [17–19]. So how to detect and treat these pollutants quickly and accurately has become the problem that researchers are paying close attention to. In recent years, many fluorescent probes have been reported to detect metal ions, anions, small organic molecules, amino acids, antibiotics etc [20–28]. Better selectivity, higher sensitivity, and detection of multiple substances have become new targets for its continuous development [29–32]. Multifunctional fluorescent probes have the characteristic that one probe can detect multiple targets, which can improve efficiency, reduce costs, and realize the detection of multiple substances, so it has important research significance [33–36]. Because of unique porous structures, adjustable frameworks and abundant bonding sites, FMOFs have become an important choice for constructing multifunctional fluorescent probes [37,38].

Recently, a number of fluorescent Ln-MOFs have been developed to detect various substances [39–41]. However, stable and multifunctional Ln-MOFs that can detect multiple substances at the same time are not common. In order to achieve this goal, the choice of ligands is particularly important. In this paper, nitrogenous carboxylic acid ligand 4,4'- (4-amino-1,2,4-triazol-3,5-diyl) dibenzoic acid (H2atdbc) was selected to construct a new lanthanide metal-organic framework. It has the following advantages: (1) The rigid ligand containing imidazole ring can improve stability of Ln-MOF. (2) The large π conjugated system can improve the luminescence characteristics. (3) The multiple coordination sites can construct a variety of structures and are helpful for detection. And as far as we know, there is only one Zn-MOF based on H2atdbc and Hbtz (1H-benzotriazole) reported, which has good water and chemical stability, and exhibits good ability to separate CO2 with CH4 [42]. Herein, a Tb-MOF based on H2atdbc was constructed. It shows good water stability and pH stability, and can be used as a multifunctional fluorescent probe to detect Fe3+, Cr2O7 2− and acetylacetone.

Tb(NO3)3·6H2O, H2atdbc and other reagents used in this experiment were purchased from commercial sources and used as received without further purification. Fluorescence spectra were measured on F-4500 fluorescence spectrophotometer (Hitachi, China) with a xenon lamp as the excitation source and glass cuvette (path length ¼ 1 ​cm). The UV–vis absorption spectra were measured on a T9S. The infrared data (IR) were measured on Nicolet iS10 with KBr pellets in the range 4000-500 ​cm-1. Powder X-ray diffraction (PXRD) patterns were recorded on an Ultima IV with Ultima IV diffractometer at 40 ​kV, 40 ​mA with Cu Kα (λ ¼ 1.5406 ​Å) radiation in the 2θ range of 15–50°. Scanning electron microscopy (SEM) observations were carried out on a Hitachi SU8010 scanning electron microscope operated at 10 ​kV. Elemental mapping were conducted using energy dispersive X-ray (EDX) detector equipped on Tecnai F30 field emission transmission electron microscope. Thermogravimetric analysis (TGA) were measured on TGA/SDTA851e in air atmosphere, heated from room temperature to 800 ​°C at a rate of 10 ​°C min-1. Fluorescence lifetime were measured on FLS1000.

4,4'-(4-Amino-1,2,4-triazol-3,5-diyl) dibenzoic acid (H2atdbc, 32.4 ​mg, 0.1 ​mmol), Tb(NO3)3·6H2O (22.7 ​mg, 0.05 ​mmol) and N,N-dimethylformamide (DMF, 8 ​mL) were put into a sealed 23.0 ​mL Teflon-lined autoclave and the pH was adjusted to 2–3 with HNO3. The mixture was heated at 170 ​°C for 72 ​h. Colorless transparent flake crystals were obtained by filtration. Then Tb-MOF could be directly filtered and washed three times alternately with DMF and deionized water then dried in air, and collected for fluorescence detections.

3 ​mg fully ground Tb-MOF samples were dispersed respectively in 3 ​mL different solutions with pH 1–14 for 24 ​h and 3 ​mL deionized water for one week. The stable suspensions were formed by ultrasonic treatment of 30 ​min, then fluorescence spectra were performed to analyze the pH stability and water stability of fluorescence of Tb-MOF. After filtration, PXRD characterization were conducted.

3 ​mg fully ground Tb-MOF samples were dispersed in 3 ​mL deionized water of M(NO3)x(M ​= ​Na+, K+, Li+, Ca2+, Sr2+, Mg2+, Cd2+, Ba2+, Zn2+, Hg2+, Ni2+, Mn2+, Co2+, Cu2+, Al3+, Cr3+, In3+, Fe3+) with the concentration of 2 ​× ​10-3 ​mol ​L-1 and KX (X ​= ​I-, Br-, Cl-, F-, SO4 2−, NO2 -, NO3 -, IO3 -, ClO3 -, Cr2O7 2−) with the concentration of 1 ​× ​10-2 ​mol ​L-1. And 3 ​mg fully ground Tb-MOF samples were introduced into various 3 ​mL organic solvents, for instance, isopropyl alcohol, cyclohexane, ethyl acetate, tetrahydrofuran (THF), dimethyl sulfoxide (DMSO), N,N-dimethylformamide (DMF), formaldehyde, N,N-Dimethylacetamide (DMA), acetone and acetylacetone. Then the above mixtures were sonicated for 30 ​min to form stable suspensions.

Infrared characterization shows that H2atdbc has a strong stretching vibration peak of -COO- at 1685 ​cm-1, and has a stretching vibration peak of -NH2 at 3350 ​cm-1. After it reacts with Tb3+, the stretching vibration peak of -COO- in H2atdbc has a blue shift about 100-150 ​cm-1, and the stretching vibration peak of –NH2 has almost unchanged, which proved coordination reactions was taken place between Tb3+ and -COO- in the H2atdbc (Fig. S1). Fig. S2 is the powder X-ray diffraction (PXRD) patterns of Tb-MOF and H2atdbc. It can be seen that there is a big difference between them, which indicating that H2atdbc has successfully coordinated with Tb3+.The main diffraction peaks of the X-ray diffraction spectrum of Tb-MOF are concentrated between 15°-35°, and the peak shape is sharp. Then the six diffraction peaks with strong diffraction intensity are marked, numbered 1–6, and the 2θ values correspond to 19.584°，20.013°，20.900°，22.433°，24.911°，32.580°, and the crystallinity is calculated as 96.93%, 97.47%, 99.05%, 98.57%, 97.97%, 99.21% (Table S1). It indicates that Tb-MOF crystallizes well.

Scanning electron microscopy observations were carried out to characterize the crystal morphology of Tb-MOF. As shown in Fig. S3 (a), Tb-MOF are flakes with a size of about 40–50 ​μm. However, the crystals of Tb-MOF are very thin, and they are easy to aggregate and grow into polycrystals. Fig. S3 (b) shows the morphology of Tb-MOF crystal clusters. The energy spectrum analysis of Tb-MOF shows the signal peaks of C, O, N and Tb elements (Fig. S3 (c) and (d)). Thermogravimetric analysis (TGA) was measured to explore the thermal stability of Tb-MOF，and it was measured in an air atmosphere and heated from room temperature to 800 ​°C at a rate of 10 ​°C·min-1. As shown in Fig. S4, the thermogravimetric curve of Tb-MOF can be maintained stable from room temperature to 315 ​°C and the weight loss in the range of 315–800 ​°C can be attributed to the collapse of the skeleton of Tb-MOF.

The luminescence properties of Tb-MOF were investigated at room temperature. As shown in Fig. 1 , Tb-MOF emitted strong green fluorescence under ultraviolet light. When excited at 325 ​nm, the emission spectrum of Tb-MOF showed four characteristic emission peaks at 493, 548, 582 and 621 ​nm, corresponding to the 5D4-7F6, 5D4-7F5, 5D4-7F4 and 5D4-7F3 characteristic transitions of Tb3+. The broad emission around 400 ​nm was the fluorescence of H2atdbc.

As shown in Fig. 2 , experimental results showed that when the pH was in the range of 3–11, the fluorescence of Tb-MOF could maintain relatively stable, and when the pH was 1–2, the fluorescence intensity decreased. Also, the fluorescence intensity of Tb-MOF immersed in deionized water for a week did not change significantly (Fig. S5). It indicates that Tb-MOF can not only be stable in deionized water for a period of time, but also can be stable in a large pH range (3–11). The PXRD results of Tb-MOF immersed in deionized water and aqueous solutions of different pH values fit well with the standard curve, which also confirms this result (Fig. S6).

3 ​mg fully ground Tb-MOF powder was immersed in 3 ​mL deionized water of M(NO3)x (M ​= ​Na+, K+, Li+, Ca2+, Sr2+, Mg2+, Cd2+, Ba2+, Zn2+, Hg2+, Ni2+, Mn2+, Co2+, Cu2+, Al3+, Cr3+, In3+, Fe3+) to form a stable suspension. The results of the fluorescence spectra showed that most metal ions have a negligible effect on the fluorescence intensity of Tb-MOF. Cu2+, Cr3+ and Co2+ could significantly weaken the fluorescence intensity of Tb-MOF. And only Fe3+ almost completely quenched the fluorescence of Tb-MOF (Fig. 3 a). Hence, Fe3+ in aqueous solution can be selectively detected by the fluorescence quenching of Tb-MOF. The competitive experiments showed that Tb-MOF still maintain high selectivity for Fe3+ in the presence of other interfering metal ions (Fig. 3b).

The sensitivity of Tb-MOF to Fe3+ detection was investigated. 3 ​mg fully ground Tb-MOF powder was dispersed into different concentration of Fe3+ aqueous solutions to monitor the correlation between Fe3+ concentration and Tb-MOF fluorescence intensity. Fig. 3c shows that as the concentration of Fe3+ increases, the fluorescence intensity of Tb-MOF decreases. When the concentration of Fe3+ increases to 2 ​× ​10-3 ​mol ​L-1, the fluorescence of Tb-MOF is almost completely quenched. The luminescence quenching efficiency is estimated by Stern-Volmer (S-V) equation I0/I ​= ​1 ​+ ​KSV[A] [43] and it is calculated to be 1.28 ​× ​104 ​L ​mol-1(Fig. 3d). And the detection limit is 5.01 ​× ​10-7 ​mol·L-1according to the equation of 3ε/S [43](Fig. S7). It is better than many MOF probes currently reported (Table S4) [14,41,44–48].

The influence of anions on the fluorescence intensity of Tb-MOF was explored. 3 ​Mg Tb-MOF powder was added into 3 ​mL different aqueous solutions of KX (X ​= ​I-, Br-, Cl-, F-, SO4 2−, NO2 -, NO3 -, IO3 -, ClO3 -, Cr2O7 2−). The experimental results showed that all anions could weaken the fluorescence of Tb-MOF to a certain extent, but only Cr2O7 2− obviously quenched the fluorescence (Fig. 4 a), which indicated that Tb-MOF could be used to detect Cr2O7 2−. As Fig. 4b shown, the presence of other anions did not interfere the detection. The cycling performance has also been studied. The dispersed Tb-MOF was centrifugated and washed with deionized water several times after the sensing experiment. Fluorescence spectrum shows that the emission intensity is basically unchanged after four repeated cycles (Fig. S8), which proved the good circulation of Tb-MOF. Fig. 4c shows emission spectra of Tb-MOF with different concentration of Cr2O7 2− solutions. It could be seen that as the concentration of Cr2O7 2− increases, the fluorescence intensity of Tb-MOF gradually decreases. Fig. 4d shows the linear correlation between the fluorescence intensity of Tb-MOF (5D4-7F5) and the concentration of Cr2O7 2−. There is a good linear relationship between relative fluorescence intensity I0/I and concentration of Cr2O7 2− (Fig. 4d). The quenching constant was calculated to 8454.37 ​L ​mol-1 and the detection limit (LOD) was 2.92 ​× ​10-6 ​mol ​L-1(Fig. S9), which reaches the current lowest level of MOF in detection of Cr2O7 2− (Table S5) [14,47,49–52].

Organic pollutants are an important part of environmental treatment. Here, 14 species common organic solvents cyclohexane, THF, HCHO, DMA, DMF, isopropyl alcohol, ethyl acetate, DMSO, acetone and acetylacetone were chosen to explore its influence on the fluorescence of Tb-MOF. As shown in Fig. 5 a, methanol has little effect and other organic solvents can weaken the fluorescence, while only acetylacetone can completely quench the fluorescence of Tb-MOF, which means that Tb-MOF can be used to selectively detect acetylacetone through the response of fluorescence quenching. And the detection of acetylacetone is not interfered by other organic solvents (Fig. 5b). Tb-MOF in acetylacetone solution (acetylacetone content: 16.67 ​vol%) after sensing experiment could be regenerated by centrifugation and washed with deionized water and methanol several times. It could be seen that after regeneration four times, the fluorescence intensity and quenching efficiency were basically unchanged (Fig. S10), which shows that Tb-MOF has good recyclability.

Considering the solubility of acetylacetone in water and methanol, methanol/H2O(v/v ​= ​1/1) was selected as solvent in the sensitivity experiment. 3 ​mg Tb-MOF was added to 3 ​mL methanol/water, and different volumes of acetylacetone were added. Fig. 5c shows that as the concentration of acetylacetone increased, the fluorescence intensity of Tb-MOF decreased gradually. When the volume of acetylacetone reached 300 ​μL, the fluorescence intensity of Tb-MOF was basically quenched. Fig. 5d shows the correlation between the relative fluorescence intensity I0/I and the concentration of acetylacetone. The quenching constant was calculated to 12.32 ​L ​mol-1 and the detection limit (LOD) was 5.59 ​× ​10-4 ​mol ​L-1 (Fig. S11), It is equivalent to the current MOF probe for detecting acetylacetone (Table S6) [53–55].

The mechanism of Tb-MOF to detect Fe3+ has the following four possibilities: (A) The analyte causes the MOF framework collapse [56]. (B) Ion exchange reactions occur between metal ions and the metal center in the MOF framework [57]. (C) Coordination occurs between the analyte and uncoordinated sites of the ligand in the MOF [58–60]. (D) The overlap of spectra causes energy transfer process [61–65]. First, the PXRD patterns of Tb-MOF before and after detection of Fe3+ are shown in Fig. S12. It is clear that the structure of Tb-MOF did not collapse. 5 ​mg fully ground Tb-MOF was soaked in 5 ​mL Fe3+ solution for 3 days, then after centrifugation, the supernatant was taken for ICP measurements. The result shows that the Fe3+ content was 72.5% and the Tb3+ content was 27.5% (Table S2), which means that ion exchange was occurred and Fe3+ replaced part of Tb3+ ions in Tb-MOF. Next, IR spectra (Fig. S13) showed that Tb-MOF did not change significantly before and after Fe3+ detection, and the vibration peak of -NH2 has no obvious change, so the -NH2 on the ligand did not coordinate with Fe3+. Finally the excitation spectrum of Tb-MOF and the UV–Vis adsorption spectra of Fe3+ was performed. As shown in Fig. 6 , the excitation spectrum of Tb-MOF and the UV–Vis adsorption spectrum of Fe3+ have a large degree of overlap, which indicates that energy transfer might occur between Fe3+ and Tb-MOF. Energy transfer process usually can cause dynamic quenching and static quenching, which could be distinguished by fluorescence lifetime. Thus, fluorescence lifetimes of Tb-MOF and Tb-MOF combined with Fe3+ were measured (Fig. S14). And the lifetime values (τ) were shown in Table S3, τ1 for Tb-MOF and Tb-MOF combined with Fe3+ are 274.4116 μs and 159.7420 ​μs, respectively, τ2 are 741.0596 μs and 398.3978 ​μs, which shows that after Fe3+ sensing experiment, τ1 and τ2 for Tb-MOF were both reduced. Hence, the quenching mechanism is considered to be the dynamic quenching [66]. Based on the above experiments, the mechanism of Tb-MOF in detection Fe3+ may be based on energy transfer and ion exchange.

Then the mechanism of Tb-MOF detection Cr2O7 2− and acetylacetone was explored. As shown in Fig. S10, the PXRD patterns of Tb-MOF after sensing Cr2O7 2− and acetylacetone are completely consistent to that of Tb-MOF itself, which proves that the structure of Tb-MOF did not collapse in the detection. The energy competition and transfer between Cr2O7 2−, acetylacetone and Tb-MOF is considered as the main reason for fluorescence quenching. Fig. 6 shows the UV–vis adsorption spectra of Cr2O7 2− and acetylacetone and the excitation spectrum of the Tb-MOF. It can be seen that the excitation spectrum of Tb-MOF has a large degree of overlap with the UV–Vis adsorption spectra of Cr2O7 2− and acetylacetone separately, which proved that the fluorescence quenching mechanism of Tb-MOF in detection Cr2O7 2− and acetylacetone can be considered as the energy transfer between Tb-MOF and Cr2O7 2−, acetylacetone.

In summary, through the rational select of the ligand, a new type of stable Tb-MOF was synthesized by solvothermal method, which can be used as a multifunctional probe to detect Fe3+ and Cr2O7 2− in water systems, and the organic solvent acetylacetone in the system of methanol/H2O/(v/v ​= ​1/1). Tb-MOF has good selectivity and sensitivity to detect Fe3+, Cr2O7 2− and acetylacetone, and the detection limits reach as low as 5.01 ​× ​10-7, 2.92 ​× ​10-6 and 5.59 ​× ​10-4 ​mol ​L-1. Meanwhile, Tb-MOF has good circulation. Through the discussion of the mechanism, it is confirmed that the detection of Fe3+ by Tb-MOF is energy transfer between Tb-MOF and Fe3+ and ion exchange, and the detection of Cr2O7 2− and acetylacetone by Tb-MOF is based on the mechanism of energy transfer between Tb-MOF and Cr2O7 2− and acetylacetone. The results of this study can provide new ideas for the synthesis of more multifunctional MOF probes.

Jiaxin Li: Conceptualization, Methodology, Data curation, Formal analysis, Investigation, Writing – original draft. Baoqiu Yu: Formal analysis, Software. Linhan Fan: Writing – review & editing. Lin Wang: Formal analysis, Software. Yichen Zhao: Formal analysis, Software. Changyan Sun: Conceptualization, Formal analysis, Supervision, Writing – review & editing, Resources, Project administration. Wenjun Li: Project administration, Resources. Zhidong Chang: Project administration, Resources.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This project is supported by National Nature Science Foundation of China (21101013).
